LPS local.auto Geometry and Performance Report

This report visualizes each frozen non-manifold dataset and compares chart.dim = "auto" with chart.dim = "local.auto" for the completed first-batch LPS run.

The chart dimension controls how many local PCA coordinates are used by the local polynomial smoother near each evaluation point. With chart.dim = "auto" , the package estimates one global chart dimension from the observed covariate matrix and then uses that same dimension for every local fit in the dataset. This is natural when the data are expected to lie near a single manifold of roughly constant intrinsic dimension.

With chart.dim = "local.auto" , the package estimates the chart dimension separately at each anchor or evaluation point, using only the local neighborhood of observed covariates available to that local fit. This is meant for non-manifold or mixed-dimensional state spaces, where one region may look one-dimensional, another may look two-dimensional, and another may have a higher effective local dimension. The report colors each dataset visualization by these per-anchor local dimensions.

Dataset Label Decoder

Each dataset heading has the form FB##. FAMILY-DETAILS-N### . The batch prefix is an audit identifier, and the dataset ID describes the frozen geometry.

  • FB## means first-batch registry item number ## . For example, FB01 is the first frozen asset in this batch.
  • LA means a local-auto evaluation asset for the LPS chart.dim = "auto" versus chart.dim = "local.auto" comparison.
  • D1 , D2 , and D3 mean VALENCIA depth-1, depth-2, and depth-3 merged dCST component systems.
  • RAW means the relative-abundance component matrix itself, row-normalized to sum to one.
  • HC means an extended homogeneous or hypercube embedding. A suffix such as Li , Lc , Gv , or Bv gives the reference component; TOP1 means the most prevalent component was used as the reference.
  • 13K-SUB means a stratified subsample from the VALENCIA 13k phylotype relative-abundance matrix.
  • SYN means a synthetic mixed-dimensional geometry, such as a surface plus a line, intersecting planes, simplex faces, or high-dimensional rank blocks.
  • N### gives the sample size. For example, N500 means 500 samples.

Example: FB01. LA-D1-RAW-N500 is first-batch item 01: a local-auto comparison asset built from the VALENCIA depth-1 dCST raw relative-abundance coordinates with 500 samples.

The summary comparison follows the Codex HTML report convention for paired method comparisons. For a synthetic dataset, the observed response is the true signal plus noise:

\[ y_i = f_i + \varepsilon_i, \qquad i = 1,\ldots,n. \]

For fitted values, the synthetic target is Truth RMSE:

\[ \operatorname{TruthRMSE}(\widehat f) = \left\{ \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \left(\widehat f_i - f_i\right)^2 \right\}^{1/2}. \]

Truth RMSE is the quantity we would minimize if the true function were known. It is available here only because these are synthetic or synthetic-truth examples; it is the oracle diagnostic used to judge whether a deployable selection rule chose a good fit.

The report also includes Observed RMSE:

\[ \operatorname{ObservedRMSE}(\widehat f) = \left\{ \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \left(\widehat f_i - y_i\right)^2 \right\}^{1/2}. \]

Observed RMSE measures how closely the selected full-data fit follows the noisy observations. It is not the synthetic target, because following the noisy observations too closely can mean interpolation or overfitting. It is used here as an interpretability and overfit diagnostic alongside Truth RMSE.

The selection score reported as CV RMSE is the fold-weighted validation RMSE:

\[ \operatorname{CVRMSE}(\theta) = \left\{ \frac{1}{n} \sum_{k=1}^{K} \sum_{i \in V_k} \left( \widehat f^{(-k)}_{\theta,i} - y_i \right)^2 \right\}^{1/2}, \]

The notation in this formula means that each validation fold is indexed by k, and the fitted value with superscript minus k is predicted from a model trained without that fold. CV RMSE is the deployable quantity: it aims to estimate Truth RMSE when the true signal is unknown, as it would be in real applications.

The paired comparison plotted below uses the dataset-level Truth-RMSE delta

\[ \Delta_j = \operatorname{TruthRMSE}_{j}(\mathrm{local.auto}) - \operatorname{TruthRMSE}_{j}(\mathrm{auto}). \]

Positive values mean that chart.dim = "local.auto" is worse than chart.dim = "auto" on the corresponding dataset. The red point and interval summarize the Bayesian-bootstrap median paired delta and its 95% credible interval.

For the per-dataset geometry views, the report also computes the exact pointwise contribution

\[ c_i = \frac{ (\widehat f_i^{\,local.auto}-f_i)^2 - (\widehat f_i^{\,auto}-f_i)^2 }{ n\{\operatorname{TruthRMSE}(local.auto) + \operatorname{TruthRMSE}(auto)\} }. \]

These components sum exactly to the dataset-level Truth-RMSE delta. A blue point has negative contribution and helps chart.dim = "local.auto" ; a red point has positive contribution and helps chart.dim = "auto" .

Summary Pairwise Comparison

Dataset Visualizations

For ambient dimension p <= 3, the widget shows the dataset in native ambient coordinates, centered and scaled. For p > 3, the widget shows vertices of an adaptive-radius kNN graph with edges hidden; the layout uses classical MDS followed by weighted KK polish when possible. Every widget colors points by the per-anchor local dimension selected by the local.auto model.

FB01. LA-D1-RAW-N500

VALENCIA depth-1 dCST; n = 500, p = 4. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 34; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

FB02. LA-D1-HC-Li-N500

VALENCIA depth-1 hypercube; n = 500, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB03. LA-D1-HC-Lc-N500

VALENCIA depth-1 hypercube; n = 500, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB04. LA-D1-HC-Gv-N500

VALENCIA depth-1 hypercube; n = 500, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB05. LA-D1-HC-Bv-N500

VALENCIA depth-1 hypercube; n = 500, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB06. LA-D2-RAW-N500

VALENCIA depth2 dCST; n = 500, p = 43. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 33; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

FB07. LA-D2-HC-TOP1-N500

VALENCIA depth-2 hypercube; n = 500, p = 42. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 35; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

FB08. LA-D3-RAW-N500

VALENCIA depth3 dCST; n = 500, p = 77. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 33; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

FB09. LA-13K-SUB-N500

VALENCIA full phylotype matrix; n = 500, p = 178. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 15; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

FB10. SYN-PARA-LINE-N500

synthetic stratified surface/curve; n = 500, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB11. SYN-SADDLE-LINE-N500

synthetic stratified surface/curve; n = 500, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB12. SYN-TWO-PLANES-N600

synthetic singular sheet union; n = 600, p = 3. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: direct ambient coordinates, p = 3.

FB13. SYN-SIMPLEX-FACES-N600

synthetic compositional strata; n = 600, p = 5. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 33; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

FB14. SYN-RANK-BLOCKS-N600-P100

synthetic high-dimensional rank strata; n = 600, p = 100. Points are colored by local.auto chart dimension.

Embedding: adaptive-radius graph layout, k.scale = 35; classical MDS initialization + weighted KK polish.

Compact Result Table

The table is included only as a compact audit aid; the figures above are the primary human-readable views.
